Return the exact source URLs used and concise supporting quotes or facts from each page.

input.thought: Start browser research on official GitHub documentation pages.

arguments.task: Inspect the public MCP servers repository page and determine whether there is an official GitHub MCP server listed or documented. Also check if GitHub has any official MCP server repository under the github organization or docs page. Return exact URLs and findings only.

input.thought: Research MCP existence via public registry/repository sources.

arguments.task: Visit the following official GitHub pages and extract concise evidence with exact URLs and short quotes/facts: 1) https://docs.github.com/en/rest/authentication/authenticating-to-the-rest-api?apiVersion=2026-03-10 for REST auth methods including fine-grained personal access tokens, GitHub App tokens, and OAuth app access tokens; 2) https://docs.github.com/en/graphql for GraphQL API existence; 3) https://docs.github.com/en/rest?apiVersion=2026-03-10 for REST API existence; 4) https://docs.github.com/en/rest/using-the-rest-api/rate-limits-for-the-rest-api?apiVersion=2026-03-10 for REST rate limits; 5) https://docs.github.com/en/graphql/overview/rate-limits-and-query-limits-for-the-graphql-api for GraphQL rate limits; 6) https://docs.github.com/en/get-started/learning-about-github/githubs-plans for plan/access availability; 7) https://github.com/github/github-mcp-server for official GitHub MCP existence. Finish with a structured summary containing only verified facts and URLs.

input.thought: Run a focused extraction pass over exact official pages to get final evidence.

raw_result.description: Code hosting and developer platform with public REST and GraphQL APIs for repositories, issues, pull requests, automation, and more.
raw_result.auth_notes: Official GitHub API docs state the REST API can be authenticated with fine-grained personal access tokens, GitHub App user/installation access tokens, and OAuth app access tokens. Personal access tokens are bearer-style tokens; OAuth app access tokens use OAuth 2.0 flows.
raw_result.gating_reason: GitHub provides self-serve accounts and public API documentation; API usage is available without partner approval, though rate limits and some higher limits depend on account/org plan context.

api_surface.breadth: Broad public REST and GraphQL surfaces covering core GitHub resources such as repositories, issues, pull requests, users, apps, actions, and more.
api_surface.mcp_notes: An official GitHub MCP server exists at github/github-mcp-server; the community MCP servers repo also points GitHub users to that official repository.

raw_result.rate_limit_notes: Official REST API rate limit docs indicate 60 requests/hour for unauthenticated requests and generally 5,000 requests/hour for authenticated requests, with 15,000/hour in certain GitHub Enterprise Cloud contexts; GitHub App installation limits can scale. Official GraphQL docs describe a point-based primary rate limit model and expose limit status via headers and the rateLimit object.
raw_result.notable_signals: Official docs clearly document both REST and GraphQL APIs, modern fine-grained PAT auth, GitHub App auth, OAuth app auth, and detailed rate limiting. GitHub also has an official MCP server repository under the github organization, which is a strong agent/tooling signal.
raw_result.human_review_reason: Access tier is best classified as self-serve free from available evidence, but exact plan requirements for all advanced API capabilities and MCP deployment details were not fully verified from the gathered pages. Pricing/access nuance may need human confirmation.
